title: refs/jjf/meta/format-version is unvalidated — delete it or actually read it
body:
# Symptom

`refs/jjf/meta/format-version` can be pointed at arbitrary
objects (including non-commit blobs) and jjf continues to work
fine. The ref appears to be dead weight in the current code
path, yet it generates the F-001/F-002/F-003 papercuts because
it participates in the push/pull/merge set.

# Repro

```
# point format-version at a random blob
git update-ref refs/jjf/meta/format-version \
  $(git hash-object -w --stdin <<<"garbage")

$ jjf ls       # 34a30c5 open  ...
$ jjf new ...  # ok=true
$ jjf show ... # works
# Nothing flags the corrupted format-version.
```

# Implications

One of:

1. **Delete the ref from the protocol.** If it isn't validated
   on read, it has no value as a sentinel. Removing it from the
   stored ref set also resolves F-001/F-002/F-003 in the
   cleanest way.
2. **Actually read it and refuse to operate on a mismatched
   version.** The point of `format-version` was to gate against
   accidental downgrades; that gate doesn't exist today. If
   we're keeping the ref, the storage layer should refuse to
   open a repo whose `format-version` it doesn't recognize.

Either choice resolves the meta/* cluster. The current
in-between state is the worst of both worlds.

# Cross-links

F-001 / F-002 / F-003 — same root cause cluster. The meta/*
fix likely lands once for all four.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100%. Tested in `scratchpad/qa/r07b-corrupt2/`.
